conditions.text: Question: A rectangular coil with 110 turns and a certain area is placed in a uniform magnetic field with a specific magnetic induction strength.
As shown in the figure, this alternating current is applied to the input of an ideal transformer, and a small light bulb \( L \), with a rated voltage of 10V, lights up normally.
The maximum voltage of this alternating current is 220V
The frequency of this alternating current is 100Hz
The turns ratio of the primary to the secondary coil of the ideal transformer is 22:1

targets.text: Which of the following statements is correct?

answer_slots.target_text: Which of the following statements is correct?
answer_slots.expected_answer: The turns ratio of the primary to the secondary coil of the ideal transformer is 22:1

sentence_segments.text: Question: A rectangular coil with 110 turns and a certain area is placed in a uniform magnetic field with a specific magnetic induction strength.
The coil rotates at a constant angular velocity around an axis perpendicular to the magnetic field, generating a sinusoidal alternating current.
As shown in the figure, this alternating current is applied to the input of an ideal transformer, and a small light bulb \( L \), with a rated voltage of 10V, lights up normally.
The resistance of the coil and wires is negligible.
Which of the following statements is correct?
The maximum voltage of this alternating current is 220V
The frequency of this alternating current is 100Hz
The turns ratio of the primary to the secondary coil of the ideal transformer is 22:1
Moving the sliding contact of the rheostat towards the d-end will dim the light bulb
